title: fabric-config schema additions — knowledge_language + default_layer_filter
description: Extend the fabric-config Zod schema with two new optional fields that drive Q3 (language policy) and Q6 (existing-MCP layer filter default). (1) `knowledge_language: z.enum(['match-existing', 'zh-CN', 'en']).optional().default('match-existing')` — controls init-scan baseline template language and zh-CN body rewrite policy. (2) `default_layer_filter: z.enum(['team', 'personal', 'both']).optional().default('both')` — fallback for `fab_plan_context` when `layer_filter` input is absent. Both fields are OPTIONAL with defaults so existing fabric-config.json files parse unchanged (backward-compat).

files.change: Add `knowledge_language: z.enum(['match-existing', 'zh-CN', 'en']).optional().default('match-existing')` and `default_layer_filter: z.enum(['team', 'personal', 'both']).optional().default('both')` to the FabricConfigSchema z.object; preserve existing fields and strict-union ClientKind

files.target: test cases — locate existing or create new
files.change: Add tests: (a) all 3 knowledge_language enum values parse OK; (b) all 3 default_layer_filter values parse OK; (c) missing-field input parses with defaults applied (match-existing / both); (d) invalid enum value rejects with Zod error; (e) existing fixtures still parse (regression)

implementation: Locate existing fabric-config tests: `find packages/shared -name '*fabric-config*.test.ts' -o -name 'schemas-roundtrip.test.ts'` (likely the latter or a colocated unit test)
If no dedicated fabric-config.test.ts exists, create `packages/shared/__tests__/fabric-config.test.ts` (or wherever the conventional location is — check sibling tests for layout)
In fabric-config.ts: add 2 new optional fields at appropriate position in the z.object literal; preserve field ordering convention
In test file: write 5 test cases covering enum values, defaults, missing fields, invalid values, and a 'previous-version fixture still parses' regression check
Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/shared build` to confirm Zod inference compiles
Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/shared test` to confirm all new tests pass and existing tests still pass
Verify coverage of new lines ≥ 90% via vitest --coverage (or equivalent)

reference.pattern: Backward-compat Zod schema extension via `.optional().default(...)` — existing fixtures unmodified
files: packages/shared/src/schemas/agents-meta.ts (sibling reference for tags field added in rc.2-prep)
reference.examples: Follow the rc.2-prep tags-field addition pattern (commit a85121a feat(schema): add tags + rename events to v2 vocabulary): same `.optional().default(...)` shape, same test conventions

criteria: `pnpm --filter @fabric/shared test` exits 0 with new tests passing
Empty/minimal fabric-config.json `{}` parses cleanly with both fields defaulted
New code coverage ≥ 90% on the 2 added field declarations + their test cases

scope_boundary: OUT OF SCOPE: api-contracts schema (TASK-003), event-ledger schema (TASK-004), init template wiring (TASK-008), monorepo's own .fabric/fabric-config.json (TASK-009). This task touches ONLY the shared schema definition + its colocated tests.

rationale.chosen_approach: Add 2 fields as `.optional().default(...)` with strict z.enum to enable backward-compat parsing while locking enum domain
alternatives_considered: Required fields (forces all existing configs to update)
decision_factors: Strict enum — invalid values caught at parse time, not runtime
Flat schema preserves consistency with sibling fields
rationale.tradeoffs: Defaults ('match-existing' / 'both') are now load-bearing — must document in docs/data-schema.md (TASK-006) so users can audit

manual_checks: Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/shared build` — must exit 0 with no Zod inference errors
Manually parse a minimal `{}` config — confirm defaults applied
Manually parse `{ knowledge_language: 'invalid' }` — confirm Zod rejection error message is clear
success_metrics: All 5 test cases (3 enum-valid + missing-defaults + invalid-rejects) pass
